description: For someone who actually wants to know what is in their kitchen without keeping a spreadsheet. A grid of items with counts. Tap an item, count goes down by one. When count hits zero the item drops into a shopping section at the bottom. Add a new item with one input at the top. No barcodes, no categories.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /pantry using useState for optimistic UI updates, Hono /api/pantry PATCH route writing to a SQLite pantry_items table with columns id, name, count, updated_at, items with count 0 selected via a WHERE clause into the shopping section, lucide-react Plus and Minus icons sized 32px for fat-finger tap targets.

name: Inbox Triage Mailbox
description: For someone whose Gmail has 12,000 unread and they keep bouncing off it. Loads twenty threads from the primary inbox. Keyboard shortcuts r, k, t map to Reply, Keep, Trash piles. Nothing sends or deletes until you press Apply at the bottom. Twenty emails resolved in about three minutes, then close the tab.
keyTech: Hono /api/inbox GET route calling gmail-find-email with maxResults 20, decisions held in a useState Map keyed by Gmail thread id until the Apply button POSTs them to /api/inbox/apply which runs gmail-modify-labels and gmail-trash-email in sequence, page route at /triage shows one thread at a time with Tailwind for layout.

name: The Refund Tracker
description: For anyone who has been told 5 to 7 business days four separate times this month. A small table of refunds in flight with vendor, amount, opened date, and status. A scheduled Automation pings you on day seven and day fourteen so a missing 80 dollars does not quietly evaporate into a customer service queue.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /refunds backed by a Hono /api/refunds CRUD route writing to a SQLite refunds table with columns id, vendor, amount_cents, opened_at, closed_at, an Automation created with create_automation running daily that emails any rows where opened_at is 7 or 14 days ago and closed_at is null.

name: Birthday Radar
description: For someone who keeps missing birthdays by a week and feels bad about it every time. Shows the next eight birthdays as a horizontal timeline with dates and names. Tap a name and a Gmail draft opens, addressed correctly, with a first sentence already filled in so you do not stare at a blank compose window for twenty minutes.
keyTech: Hono /api/birthdays route reading a JSON file at /home/workspace/Records/birthdays.json with name, email, month, day fields, sorted by next occurrence from today's date, click handler calls gmail-create-draft with a templated body, page route at /birthdays renders a horizontal scroll row using Tailwind flex and gap utilities.

name: The Grandma Letter Page
description: For someone who means to write their grandmother more often and does not. Once a month a quiet email arrives with one prompt and a link to the page. Write one paragraph, hit save. The archive grows month over month into something worth printing later, which is the actual point of the whole thing.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /letters with a single textarea and a Save button, Hono /api/letters POST writing to a SQLite letters table with columns id, recipient, body, written_at, a monthly Automation created with create_automation using rrule FREQ=MONTHLY;BYMONTHDAY=1 that sends a reminder via send_email_to_user with the page link.

name: Closet Inventory Page
description: For someone who owns more clothes than they remember and keeps buying the same black t-shirt. Snap a photo from your phone, tag it with one word like shirt or boots, and it lands in a scrollable grid. Filter by dominant color when nothing in the closet seems wearable. Honest about what you actually own.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /closet with a file input POSTing to a Hono /api/closet/upload route that calls update_space_asset to store images at /closet-images/<id>.jpg, metadata row written to a SQLite closet_items table with columns id, asset_path, tag, dominant_color, color filter rendered as a Tailwind flex row of swatch buttons.

name: The Subscription Wall
description: For the person who suspects they are paying for at least three things they do not use. Every recurring charge listed by monthly cost, sorted high to low, with the next renewal date beside each. A red dot appears next to anything not opened or logged in for 60 days. Cancel candidates become obvious in one scroll.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /subscriptions reading a SQLite subscriptions table with columns id, name, monthly_cents, renews_on, last_used_at, last_used_at updated manually or via a tiny Hono /api/ping/:id endpoint you can curl from a browser bookmark, red dot rendered as a 6px lucide-react Circle when now minus last_used_at exceeds 60 days.

name: Tonight's One Thing
description: For someone whose to-do list has become a monument to failure. Each morning the page shows one input and one question: what would make tonight feel okay. Type a sentence, press enter, done. The page archives every answer with its date. Reading the list back a month later is the surprise reward of the whole thing.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /tonight with a single text input, Hono /api/tonight POST writing to a SQLite daily_intents table with a unique date column plus text and written_at, page checks today's date on load via useEffect and either renders the input or the answer already submitted, prior entries listed below in a muted column.

name: Doctor Visit Notes
description: A short form you fill out in the parking lot after a doctor's appointment, before the details blur. Date, doctor name, three things they said, what you decided to do about it. Lands you with a searchable record you can scroll through before the next visit instead of trying to remember whether the cardiologist actually said 'cut back' or 'cut out.'
keyTech: zo.space page route at /notes with a controlled form, POSTs to a Hono /api/notes route that appends to a JSON file at /home/workspace/Records/doctor-notes.json keyed by date, list view renders newest-first with Tailwind for layout

name: The Borrowed-Things Ledger
description: Two columns of who has your stuff and what you have of theirs, with the date it left or arrived. Lands you with a private ledger that answers 'wait, where is my drill' without an argument. After thirty days an Automation drafts a low-key reminder text you can choose to send or ignore.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /borrowed with two-column Tailwind layout, Hono /api/items POST and PATCH routes writing to a JSON file at /home/workspace/Records/borrowed.json, Automation runs daily via create_automation and flags any row where loanedAt is older than 30 days

name: Family Recipe Card Wall
description: A public grid of recipes that look like index cards from your own kitchen, not a food blog. Click a card and it flips to show the method on the back. Lands a visitor on the recipe, not on three paragraphs about your grandmother's porch. No ads, no popup, no scroll-to-the-bottom routine.
keyTech: zo.space page route reading markdown files from /home/workspace/Recipes/, Hono /api/recipes GET route returns parsed frontmatter and body as JSON, card flip uses a CSS transform with useState, photos uploaded once with update_space_asset and referenced by slug

name: The Quiet Receipt Page
description: Private page that takes a phone photo of a paper receipt, runs OCR against it, names the file by date and merchant, and files it away. Lands a freelancer at tax time with a folder of named PNGs and a CSV of amounts instead of a shoebox. Do not try to categorize spending here; that is a different page.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /receipts with a file input, Hono /api/receipts POST route writes the image to /home/workspace/Records/receipts/YYYY/ and appends a row to receipts.csv with date, merchant, amount, filename; OCR via a Tesseract call in the route handler

name: Permission Slip Generator
description: Private page for a parent who has filled out the same field trip form four times this year. Pick a kid from a dropdown, pick or type the trip, click generate, get a printable PDF with the signature line ready. Lands you with a folder of past slips so the next teacher who asks gets one in under a minute.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /slips with a Hono /api/slip POST route that builds the PDF with jsPDF server-side, kids and emergency contacts read from a JSON file at /home/workspace/Records/family.json, generated PDFs written to /home/workspace/Records/permission-slips/

name: Pet Medication Schedule
description: A grid showing which dog gets which pill on which day, with a check-off button big enough to hit from a phone while your hands are full. Lands a household with two animals on different schedules on the same page so nobody double-doses Lucky because they forgot the other person already did it.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /pills with a weekly grid built in Tailwind, Hono /api/pills POST route writes check-offs to a SQLite file at /home/workspace/Records/pets.db with a doses table (pet, medication, date, checked_by), Automation sends an SMS via send_sms_to_user if a dose is still unchecked by 9pm

name: The Permission-to-Rest Page
description: A page that does almost nothing on purpose. It says, in serious typography, that you are allowed to rest today, and shows the current date so you remember when you needed it enough to open the page. Lands a tired person with one sentence and a timestamp. No journaling prompt, no streak counter, no follow-up.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /rest, single h1 in Instrument Serif at 4rem on a warm off-white background, date rendered with toLocaleDateString in the user's timezone, no state, no API route, no database; the visit itself is logged to a file at /home/workspace/Records/rest-days.txt via a Hono /api/rest-log POST route

name: Funeral RSVP Page
description: A public single page for a memorial. Time, place, parking notes, who to call if you get lost, and a yes-or-no RSVP. Lands a grieving family with a link they can text to relatives without explaining anything. No emojis, no banner image, no music. The page is allowed to be quiet.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /memorial with details rendered from a JSON file at /home/workspace/Records/memorial.json, Hono /api/rsvp POST route appends name, attending, plus_ones, note to rsvps.json, list of confirmed names visible only on a private /memorial/admin route gated by bearer auth

name: The Lawn-Mowing Memory
description: One button. You tap it when you mow the lawn. The page shows how many days since the last cut and a rough prediction of the next one based on the month. Lands you with a private log so you can stop arguing with yourself about whether it has really been two weeks or only ten days.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /lawn with a single button that POSTs to a Hono /api/lawn route, mow dates appended to a JSON file at /home/workspace/Records/lawn.json, next-cut prediction is a lookup table by month (7 days in June, 14 in September) rendered with useState on load

name: The Soft Goodbye Page
description: A public page you send to a friend who is leaving the company or the city. They write notes on it over a few days, you read them later. Lands the friend in a soft text box, not a Slack thread that scrolls away by Tuesday. One page, one person, no comment threading, no reactions.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /goodbye/:slug with a textarea that auto-saves on blur, Hono /api/goodbye/:slug POST route appends timestamped entries to a markdown file at /home/workspace/Records/goodbyes/{slug}.md, slug generated when you create the page and shared as a direct link

name: The 'Did I Eat' Tracker
description: Three buttons: breakfast, lunch, dinner. That is the whole interface. Over a month a small calendar fills in so you can see the shape of your eating without anyone scoring it. Lands a person who forgets to eat with a quiet record, not a calorie tracker. Do not add a notes field; the point is the friction is gone.
keyTech: zo.space page route at /eat with three Tailwind buttons that POST to a Hono /api/eat route, taps written to a SQLite file at /home/workspace/Records/eat.db with a meals table (date, meal, tapped_at), calendar view queries the same table grouped by date

name: The Power Outage Page
description: A bare public page for a household to point at when the power goes out and nobody can remember the utility's number. Phone numbers, who to call first, where the flashlights live, when the last outage was and how long it lasted. Lands a flustered family member on the four things they actually need. Works on a phone with one bar.
keyTech: zo.space page route at / with content read from a markdown file at /home/workspace/Records/outage.md via a Hono /api/outage GET route, outage history table rendered from outages.json (started, ended, duration), no JavaScript beyond initial fetch so it loads on a weak connection

name: Pre-Commit Hook Inspector
description: For the developer joining a new codebase who does not want to discover the rules by getting their first commit rejected. Paste a public repo URL, get back every pre-commit hook configured, the files each one inspects, and a sample of which staged changes would trip them. Leave with a checklist of formatters, linters, and secret scanners to install locally before opening a branch.
keyTech: Hono `/api/inspect` POST route shallow-clones the repo to `/tmp/zo-hook-inspector/<sha>` with `git clone --depth 1`, parses `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `.husky/`, and `.git/hooks/` into a JSON shape, page route at `/` renders the result with React and Tailwind, inspections cached for one hour in a SQLite `inspections` table at `/home/workspace/Records/hook-inspector.sqlite`.

name: The Stale PR Wall
description: For the solo maintainer or small team lead who keeps meaning to follow up on their own pull requests. Shows every PR you opened that has sat without a review comment for more than seven days, sorted by how long the silence has lasted. Click any row to draft a short, polite nudge written in your voice. Leave with a triage list and three nudges already in the clipboard.
keyTech: Page route at `/stale-prs`, Hono `/api/prs` GET hits the GitHub GraphQL search API with `is:open author:@me`, results cached to `/home/workspace/Records/stale-prs.json` with a 30 minute TTL, nudge drafts generated via the Zo Ask API and rendered in a Tailwind two column layout, GitHub token read from `process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN`.

name: Stripe Refund Reason Histogram
description: For the indie founder who suspects their churn story and their actual refund reasons do not match. Reads the last 90 days of Stripe refunds, groups by the `reason` field plus free text in `metadata.customer_reason`, and renders a bar chart. Leave with the actual top three things customers ask their money back for, not the version you tell yourself in standups.
keyTech: Hono `/api/refunds` GET pages through `stripe.refunds.list({ limit: 100 })` with `process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` until the 90 day cutoff, aggregates counts into a JSON cache at `/home/workspace/Records/refund-histogram.json`, page route renders a Recharts bar chart and a small table of the long tail reasons under a count threshold of three.

name: The Bus-Factor Page
description: For the tech lead on a small team who has not said the words `bus factor` out loud yet but is starting to feel it. Looks at the last 90 days of commits across selected repos and flags every file only one author has touched, ranked by how often that file changes. Leave with a short list of files that need a second pair of eyes before someone takes a vacation.
keyTech: Hono `/api/bus` GET walks the GitHub commits API for each repo in a config array, results stored in a SQLite `file_authors` table at `/home/workspace/Records/bus-factor.sqlite` with columns `repo, path, author, last_touched, change_count`, page route renders a Tailwind table grouped by repo with a per-repo refresh button that triggers a re-crawl.

name: Cold DM Composer
description: For the founder who has to send cold outreach and refuses to open with `Hope you are doing well`. Paste a person's bio and a one sentence ask, get back three short opener drafts written in your voice, each from a different angle: specific compliment, mutual context, blunt question. Leave with one you can send and two you can steal lines from.
keyTech: Page route at `/dm` with a two field form, Hono `/api/draft` POST sends the bio plus ask to the Zo Ask API with a system prompt that bans seven hedging phrases, response parsed via `output_format` into a three-string `openers` array, every generated draft appended to `/home/workspace/Records/dm-drafts.jsonl` for later review.

name: Linear Issue Smell Test
description: For the engineering manager who is tired of writing the same review comment on every ticket. POST a Linear issue ID with a bearer token, get a JSON critique of the title, description, acceptance criteria, and scope honesty, each scored 1 to 5 with one sentence of reasoning. Leave with a number you can paste into a comment and a short list of what is missing.
keyTech: Hono POST `/api/smell-test` route gated by a `LINEAR_SMELL_TEST_TOKEN` bearer check, pulls the issue via the Linear GraphQL API with `process.env.LINEAR_API_KEY`, hands the body to the Zo Ask API with `output_format` set to a four field rubric, every request and response appended to `/home/workspace/Records/smell-test-log.jsonl`.

name: API Latency Honesty Page
description: For the indie operator whose status page says `all systems operational` and whose users keep saying otherwise. Pings your selected endpoints from five public probe locations every fifteen minutes, then shows the p50, p95, and worst single request per endpoint per day. Leave with the real numbers, including the embarrassing ones you did not want to graph.
keyTech: Automation created with `create_automation` runs every 15 minutes and calls Hono `/api/probe` for each endpoint via Bun fetch from regional proxy URLs, results inserted into a SQLite `pings` table at `/home/workspace/Records/latency.sqlite` with columns `endpoint, region, ts, ms, status`, page route renders a Recharts line and a daily worst case row.

name: Indie Hacker Pricing A/B Page
description: For the indie maker who wants to test two prices without a third party tag, a cookie banner, or another SaaS subscription. The same route serves variant A or B based on a stable hash of the visitor IP, tracks which variant led to a Stripe checkout, and shows a small admin panel with conversion by variant. Leave with one variant winning, or honest data showing neither does.
keyTech: Page route at `/pricing` picks the variant via a SHA256 hash of `x-forwarded-for` modulo 2 in a `useState` initializer, Hono `/api/track` POST records `variant, event, ts` rows in a SQLite `ab_events` table at `/home/workspace/Records/pricing-ab.sqlite`, conversion attribution via the `client_reference_id` field on the Stripe checkout session.

name: The Changelog Comparison Page
description: For the maintainer who has to write release notes and would rather not scroll through 80 merged PRs by hand. Paste a repo and two release tags, get every merged PR title between them grouped by author and by label (feature, fix, chore, breaking). Leave with a draft changelog you can trim down instead of a blank page and a deadline.
keyTech: Page route with a two-input form, Hono `/api/compare` GET calls the GitHub `compare/{base}...{head}` endpoint then enriches each PR via the pulls API for labels, grouped results returned as JSON and rendered in a Tailwind three column layout, last 20 comparisons cached to `/home/workspace/Records/changelog-cache.json` keyed by `repo:base:head`.

name: The Cron Drift Reporter
description: For the operator who has noticed their `runs at midnight` job is somehow firing at 12:11 and has been quietly accepting it. Every Automation reports its actual start time to a heartbeat endpoint. The page shows scheduled time, actual time, drift in seconds, and which jobs have drifted more than a minute three days running. Leave knowing which schedules to rewrite or kill.
keyTech: Hono `/api/heartbeat` POST writes `job_name, scheduled_at, actual_at, drift_seconds` rows to a SQLite `runs` table at `/home/workspace/Records/cron-drift.sqlite`, each Automation calls the endpoint via Bun fetch as its first step with `process.env.HEARTBEAT_TOKEN`, page route at `/cron-drift` queries the last 14 days and renders a Tailwind table sorted by worst average drift.

name: The Open Graph Auditor
description: For the marketer or founder who keeps hearing `your link looks broken in Slack` and does not know which page is the offender. Paste a domain, the API pulls every URL from `sitemap.xml`, reads the Open Graph and Twitter card tags, and renders a grid of preview cards matching how Slack, X, and LinkedIn each draw them. Leave with a list of pages missing a title, image, or description.
keyTech: Hono `/api/og` POST takes a domain, fetches `https://<domain>/sitemap.xml` via Bun fetch, parses each `<loc>` and pulls OG plus Twitter card tags with a small regex-based HTML parser, page route renders three React preview components per URL styled to match each platform's unfurl, results cached for 24 hours in a JSON file at `/home/workspace/Records/og-audit.json`.

name: The Postmortem Folder Page
description: For the engineer or operator who writes incident postmortems and then never opens them again. Reads every markdown file from a postmortems folder, parses the frontmatter (date, duration, one sentence cause, lesson), and shows them in reverse chronological order. Click any row to read the full writeup. Leave with a feeling for which failure modes keep showing up across quarters.
keyTech: Page route at `/postmortems`, Hono `/api/postmortems` GET reads every `.md` file under `/home/workspace/Documents/Postmortems/` via `fs.readdir`, splits YAML frontmatter with a small regex parser into `{ slug, date, duration, cause, lesson }`, returns a JSON array sorted by date desc, body rendered with `react-markdown` on a `/postmortems/:slug` route at request time.

name: The Friday Demo Page
description: Internal tool for a small team that hates status meetings. Each person drops a Loom link, a screenshot, or two sentences by Friday noon. The page renders the week as five tiles, one per teammate, with whatever they posted. Managers leave knowing what shipped without booking a call. Do not turn this into a project tracker. It is a週ly demo wall, nothing more.
keyTech: Hono POST /api/demos route writes to a SQLite `demos` table (id, author, week_iso, kind, url, note, created_at), page route at `/` queries the current ISO week and renders a 5-column Tailwind grid, drop zone uses a file input plus a Loom URL field, lucide-react Video and FileText icons per tile

name: Webhook Replay Console
description: For the developer staring at a failed Stripe or GitHub webhook and wanting to fire it again without Postman. Every incoming POST is captured with full headers and body, listed newest first, and replayable against a local URL with one button. The page is private, so the capture URL is the only public surface. Do not point production webhooks at this. It is a debugging tool for staging keys.
keyTech: Hono catch-all `/api/hook/:slug` route stores raw body, headers JSON, method, and received_at into a SQLite `webhooks` table, replay endpoint reads a row and does a Bun fetch to the target URL with original headers minus Host, page route at `/replay` uses useState for the target URL input and shows response status plus body inline

name: Repo Onboarding Map
description: For the new hire on day one who has been handed a 400-file repo and a Slack channel. Paste a GitHub URL, get one screen back: the five biggest top-level folders, the ten files with the most commits in the last 90 days, and the top author per folder as the person to ask. Public so it works on open source repos without auth. Read-only by design.
keyTech: Hono `/api/map?repo=owner/name` route hits the GitHub REST API for `/contents`, `/commits?since=`, and `/stats/contributors`, caches the JSON response at `/home/workspace/Records/repo-maps/{owner}-{name}.json` for 24 hours, page route at `/` renders three Tailwind columns with lucide-react Folder, FileCode, and User icons
